intake.finding: Clear, recurring developer problem with identified owners. The explainer by the Blink Layout Team names the job (fit lines of text to container width by scaling font-size instead of justification), the segments (publishing/headlines/dynamic content), and the existing workarounds (manual tuning or JavaScript) [s1,s2]. The ChromeStatus entry was created 2025-04-16 by tkent with owners tkent/ikilpatrick/kojii and an external proposer, Roman Komarov (kizu, DataDog) [s1,s7]. Demand is traceable to a long-standing CSSWG issue [s10].
intake.limitations: No public end-user or customer research is recorded; the TAG review states user research is 'No public one' [s7].

incubation.finding: CSSWG resolved (1 Apr 2025 F2F) to add the feature and later to place it in CSS Text Level 5 [s10,s12]; web-dev signal is strongly positive (110+ votes; 83 ChromeStatus stars) [s1,s10]; the explainer includes goals, non-goals, alternatives and adjacent-platform/JS-workaround analysis [s2,s23]. However, material design disagreements with the proposer/co-editor stayed unresolved through incubation: grow-only vs grow+shrink, full font-size-relative scaling vs implementation-dependent, and a default 200% grow limit [s13,s14,s15]. The accessibility concern (WCAG 1.4.4) was raised immediately at prototype and persisted [s5,s11]. No attributable production partner/trial commitment exists [s7].
incubation.limitations: Incubation secured strong demand and a standards home but left the core design questions and the central accessibility question open.

prototype.finding: Intent to Prototype was sent 2025-04-24 [s5]; a Blink implementation landed in Blink>Layout>Inline with wpt_internal tests [s1,s7], and the Chrome team posted prototype feedback that the proposer publicly contested [s13,s15]. The prototype did not resolve the proposer's objections on the scaling model or the default limit, and at TAG-review time WPT tests were 'Not merged yet' [s7,s15].
prototype.limitations: No friction log, framework-integration spike, or representative-cohort evidence surfaced; prototype validation was informal (proposer + CSSWG/blog), not a structured task-based trial.

developerTrials.finding: No structured developer trial (behind a flag) or Origin Trial was conducted: the only recorded launch event is M150 enabled-by-default, and ChromeStatus has no dev-trial/OT stage, no flag, and null devtrial instructions [s1]. Informal validation existed via Canary prototype testing by the proposer and CSSWG/blog discussion [s14,s15]. Rationale for not-relevant: the feature deliberately skipped a formal trial cohort, which is a process state rather than an untested claim; informal feedback was gathered but not through a representative developer trial.
developerTrials.limitations: Absence of a trial means there is no pre-ship task-completion, friction, framework-integration, or field evidence.

wideReview.finding: TAG review was requested (w3ctag#1208, in progress) but was 'Issues open / no feedback for a month' at intent-to-ship and was never closed [s1,s6,s7]. Gecko and WebKit standards-position issues were filed (mozilla#1377, WebKit#637) but both remain 'No signal' with no position taken [s8,s9]; an API owner (Bratell) publicly noted disappointment at the lack of other-engine engagement [s6]. CSSWG agreed to add the feature and place it in css-text-5 [s10,s12]; web-dev is strongly positive [s1,s10]. Accessibility review is open across CSSWG and ARIA WG with no consensus [s6,s11].
wideReview.limitations: Wide review was initiated but produced no other-engine support, an unclosed TAG review, and an unresolved accessibility position.

experiment.finding: There was no Origin Trial (origintrial=false; no OT stage/id/feedback) and no field experiment [s1]. The team's rationale (new additive CSS property, no default behavior change, low compat risk) makes a trial defensible, but it forgoes field evidence on the one genuinely uncertain dimension: real-world accessibility/zoom behavior [s6,s11]. Rationale for not-relevant: a field experiment was a deliberate non-choice rather than an untested dimension; the accepted substitute is post-ship monitoring [s6].
experiment.limitations: No field-exposure or field-a11y data was collected before default-on shipment.

prepareToShip.finding: Intent to Ship was sent 2025-05-07 and received 3 LGTMs (Bratell, Taylor, Byers) after a rigorous accessibility exchange; an API owner required the a11y rationale, perf tradeoff, and monitoring plan to be captured in ChromeStatus 'Anticipated spec changes', which was done [s1,s6]. Spec text was merged to css-text-5 (PR #13616, 2026-04-20) [s3,s4]; the WPT path was linked; rollout is enabled for all on desktop/Android/WebView/M150 [s1,s6]. Gaps remained: TAG review still open; MDN reference page absent; no CanIUse entry; other-engine 'No signal' not flagged as a risk [s7,s8,s9,s19,s20].
prepareToShip.limitations: The ship packet was evidence-backed and well-questioned but shipped with a known open accessibility issue, missing reference docs, and no cross-engine support.

release.finding: Shipped enabled-by-default in Chrome 150 stable on 2026-06-30 (desktop/Android/WebView; iOS null) [s1,s16,s17,s18,s21,s22,s27]. Communication was coordinated and accurate: New in Chrome 150 (headline feature), web.dev 'New to the web platform in June', release notes, and a CodePen demo; claims match the actual rollout [s17,s18].
release.limitations: About 3 weeks post-stable; early usage telemetry is now available (see the adoption phase; s28) but support/regression data is still pending; MDN and CanIUse remain absent [s19,s20].

adoption.finding: Early but measurable adoption. The `text-fit` CSS property use counter (bucket 924; property-scope; exact-title match to the feature; no WebDX feature/use-counter-enum mapping or origin-trial counter exists) first recorded a positive value on 2026-01-23 at 0.000003 (0.0003%) of measured page loads — before M150 stable, consistent with pre-stable channel availability and/or counter detection of the property token [s28]. It peaked at 0.003303 (0.3303%) on 2026-06-03 (~4 weeks before stable), then measured 0.00058 (0.058%) at M150 stable (2026-06-30) [s28]. By the latest point (2026-07-21, ~3 weeks post-stable) it had risen to 0.00091596 (0.0916%) [s28]. The post-stable share has trended up (~+58% relative versus the at-stable point) but remains below the pre-stable peak and below 0.1% of measured page loads; day30/day90/day180 checkpoints are not yet available [s28]. No framework/library integration, attributable production deployment, or case study has been found [s1].
adoption.limitations: Property-scope counter: it fires on any measured page load where `text-fit` is used or detected and is not equivalent to unique sites, developers, users, successful tasks, production deployment, or end-user benefit [s28]. Only ~3 weeks post-stable; the pre-stable peak (0.3303%) is unexplained and may reflect pre-stable-channel experimentation, a transient demo/probe, or low-volume noise. No predeclared adoption target exists, so the level is described, not judged; Baseline/other-engine cycle still pending.

support.finding: Too early to assess. No recurring support-issue taxonomy, regressions, or compatibility reports are observable yet; the team committed to monitor accessibility in practice [s1,s6].
support.limitations: Temporal gap; support burden and issue-resolution patterns are not yet visible.

deprecation.finding: This is a new additive CSS property with no removal or deprecation path in scope [s1]. Rationale for not-relevant: deprecation does not apply to a greenfield feature launch; included for lifecycle completeness only.

developerValue.finding: Addresses a genuine, long-standing, recurring developer problem (responsive fit-to-width typography) previously solved only with manual tuning or fragile JavaScript/registered-custom-property hacks; web-dev signal is strongly positive (110+ votes, 83 stars) with an influential CSS author (kizu) as proposer [s1,s2,s10,s23]. The native declarative solution is a clear value over JS workarounds (avoids layout thrash and flash-of-unstyled-content) [s2].
developerValue.limitations: Value is partly undercut by unresolved design disagreements with the proposer over the scaling model's power and defaults [s13,s15].

endUserImpact.finding: Stated end-user benefits (no reflow 'jump', better first contentful paint vs JS) are plausible but unmeasured [s2]. A real end-user-control risk exists: when the fitted container's inline size depends on the viewport, full-page zoom may not change the rendered text size at all, potentially failing WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text; shipped without a default safeguard and with the issue open [s3,s6,s11,s14].
endUserImpact.limitations: No field accessibility evidence either way yet; benefits are claimed, not measured.

adoption.finding: Unscored: no predeclared adoption target appears in the explainer, the Intent to Prototype/Ship, or the launch materials, and no unique-site, production-deployment, retention, or case-study evidence has been found [s1,s2,s6]. The only available signal is the property-scope CSS use counter (bucket 924): 0.00058 (0.058%) of measured Chrome page loads at M150 stable (2026-06-30) and 0.00091596 (0.0916%) at the latest point (2026-07-21, ~3 weeks post-stable), with day30/day90/day180 checkpoints pending [s28]. The post-stable trend is upward but the level is low and the pre-stable peak (0.003303 / 0.3303% on 2026-06-03) is unexplained. With no threshold and no corroborating production/retention evidence, the adoption outcome is described, not scored.
adoption.limitations: Property-scope counter only; not unique sites, developers, users, successful tasks, satisfaction, or end-user benefit [s28]. ~3 weeks post-stable; no declared target; no production/retention/case-study evidence; day30/90/180 pending.

interoperability.finding: A standards home exists: CSSWG agreed to add the feature and placed it in CSS Text Level 5 (editors' draft / Working Draft, not Candidate Recommendation) [s3,s4,s10,s12]. But at launch it is Chromium-only: Gecko and WebKit have 'No signal' and no implementation, the TAG review is unclosed, and it is not Baseline [s1,s7,s8,s9,s18]. An API owner flagged disappointment at absent other-engine engagement [s6].
interoperability.limitations: Standards agreement is positive but cross-engine support is absent; the Chrome-only period was not flagged in launch materials.

implementationQuality.finding: A Blink implementation shipped with WPT coverage (css/css-text/text-fit) and DevTools deemed sufficient; it is additive and compat-safe by default [s1,s6,s24]. But the scaling model is partly implementation-dependent (optical sizing makes scaling non-proportional; the spec says the exact method is 'up to the implementation') and diverges from the proposer's desired full font-size-relative scaling, and it shipped with an open behavior (accessibility) the team explicitly expects may change [s3,s4,s11,s13,s15].
implementationQuality.limitations: Detailed WPT pass/fail and the tracking-bug contents could not be retrieved (JS-rendered/auth-gated) [s24,s25]; the implementation is functional but not spec-stable.

evidenceAndCommunication.finding: Strong pre-ship evidence and comms: an explainer, normative spec, transparent intent threads with rigorous API-owner accessibility questioning, and accurate coordinated launch assets (release notes, New in Chrome 150, web.dev summary, demo) [s2,s3,s5,s6,s16,s17,s18]. Weak or missing: the TAG review was never closed and 'No signal' from other engines was not surfaced as a risk in messaging; the MDN reference page and CanIUse entry were absent at launch; and the co-editor publicly reported near-silence on his proposed safeguards [s7,s8,s9,s14,s15,s19,s20].
evidenceAndCommunication.limitations: Launch claims were accurate, but discoverability and cross-engine-positioning gaps remain.

support.finding: Too early to score: no support burden, issue taxonomy, or resolution patterns are observable within roughly 3 weeks of stable [s1].

overall.finding: A well-motivated, well-documented, compat-safe CSS feature with strong developer demand and clean launch communication, but it shipped Chromium-only (no Gecko/WebKit signal, unclosed TAG review, not Baseline), with an open multi-WG accessibility issue and no default safeguard, unresolved design disagreements with its own proposer/co-editor, no formal developer or origin trial, and missing MDN/CanIUse at stable. Adoption telemetry now exists but is unscored (no predeclared target): the `text-fit` property counter measured 0.00058 (0.058%) of page loads at M150 stable and 0.00091596 (0.0916%) ~3 weeks later, with day30/90/180 pending [s28]; end-user impact remains mixed on an open accessibility risk. The overall status deliberately does not hide the mixed interop, accessibility, and documentation dimensions, nor the unscored adoption and end-user-impact outcomes.
overall.limitations: Post-launch outcome dimensions remain early: adoption is measurable but unscored (no target), and this is still substantially a launch-time assessment.

supports: Feature metadata, owners, finch name CssTextFit, shipped milestones (desktop/android/webview 150, ios null), web-dev strongly positive (110+ votes), Gecko/WebKit No signal, TAG review open, WPT link, spec link, bug link, motivation and interop/a11y risk text
sources.limitations: Cached accurate_as_of 2026-07-17; the cached status field still shows 'Proposed' and is_released=false although M150 stable shipped 2026-06-30 (status field lags).

supports: Problem statement, use cases (expand/shrink), goals and non-goals, considered alternatives, performance/UX/authoring rationale versus JS workarounds, proposed text-fit syntax
sources.limitations: Early design sketch; marked 'not approved to ship'; some sections (e.g., combining behaviors, fit-method) later removed/revised.

supports: Normative text-fit definition (none|grow|shrink + consistent|per-line|per-line-all + percentage limit), scalable-parts model, processing order, open accessibility note referencing csswg#12886, implementation-dependent scaling note
sources.limitations: Editors' draft / Working Draft (maturity val 4), not Candidate Recommendation; subject to change.

supports: Spec text introducing text-fit added to css-text (commit 537f46d, merged 2026-04-20); which properties use the 'used' font-size
sources.limitations: Diff view; reflects a point-in-time spec state.

supports: Intent to Prototype with motivation, Blink component, web-dev strongly positive (90+ votes at the time), Gecko/WebKit 'will file', Alan Stearns raising accessibility concerns the same day, kizu feedback blog link
sources.limitations: Single thread; does not cover later design changes.

supports: Intent to Ship (2026-05-07) with motivation, TAG 'Issues open / no feedback for a month', open a11y issue 12886, ship enabled for all on desktop/android/webview M150, 3 LGTMs (Bratell, Taylor, Byers), Alison Maher accessibility challenge, Chris Harrelson on ARIA/CSSWG discussion and perf cost, monitoring plan, Bratell disappointment at other-engine silence
sources.limitations: API-owner LGTMs are Chromium process approvals, not standards or DevRel approval.

supports: TAG review in progress / 'Issues open', review type 'later review', topic accessibility+CSS, WPT 'Not merged yet' at request time, 'No public' user research, contacts tkent-google and kizu
sources.limitations: Review never closed; no TAG position text was issued.

supports: Mozilla standards-position request filed; position remains 'No signal'; notes CSSWG agreed to add the feature; Bugzilla 2038425 referenced
sources.limitations: No Mozilla position taken; no Gecko implementation signal.

supports: WebKit standards-position request filed; position remains 'No signal'; notes CSSWG agreed to add the feature
sources.limitations: No WebKit position taken; no Safari implementation signal.

supports: Original feature request (long-standing), CSSWG resolution to add the feature (1 Apr 2025 F2F), 110+ developer votes, accessibility discussion started by Patrick H. Lauke
sources.limitations: Issue body rendered as navigation chrome when fetched; key facts (votes, resolution, a11y discussion) corroborated by s1, s5, s6, s12, s14. Approximate open date.

supports: Open accessibility issue on default scaling limit vs WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text; kizu's 200% default proposal; multi-WG (CSSWG, ARIA WG) discussion with no consensus; Chris Harrelson's proposed solution
sources.limitations: Issue body not directly parsed; substance corroborated by intent thread s6, the spec note s3, and kizu's posts s14,s15.

supports: CSSWG RESOLVED to place the feature in CSS Text Level 5 (IRC log)
sources.limitations: Exact meeting date inferred from issue context.

supports: Design disagreement on scaling of font-size-based values; Chrome team argues for implementation-dependent scaling; kizu strongly opposes; quotes Chrome prototype feedback
sources.limitations: Captures proposer's framing; engine-implementation detail not independently verified.

supports: Detailed explainer feedback: grow-only preference, WCAG 1.4.4 problem (viewport dependency), proposed default 200% grow limit (relaxed/strict), syntax and fit-method critiques
sources.limitations: Single influential author's perspective; not representative developer research.

supports: Post-prototype status: kizu disagrees with Chrome team on scaling model, grow/shrink, and default limit; reports near-silence on his proposed safeguards; co-editor intent
sources.limitations: Author's subjective account of engagement.

supports: Chrome 150 stable release notes (2026-06-30); text-fit listed as enabled-by-default; summary matches ChromeStatus record
sources.limitations: Vendor-published; descriptive only.

supports: New in Chrome 150 blog lists text-fit as a headline feature; includes CodePen demo; accurate claims matching rollout
sources.limitations: Vendor marketing/blog; no interop or adoption claims.

supports: 'New to the web platform in June' lists text-fit under Chrome 150 only with no Baseline status (contrast: field-sizing became Baseline Newly available); confirms no Safari stable in June and Firefox 152 unrelated
sources.limitations: Vendor-published; Baseline status inferred by absence.

supports: MDN reference page for text-fit returns 'Page not found' (404) at retrieval; no dedicated MDN documentation at/after stable launch
sources.limitations: Absence at a single retrieval; page may be authored later.

supports: No dedicated CanIUse feature entry for text-fit at retrieval (search resolves to unrelated fit-content/text entries)
sources.limitations: Absence at a single retrieval; entry may be added later.

supports: Independent listing of Chrome 150 features shows text-fit enabled-by-default
sources.limitations: Third-party mirror of ChromeStatus data.

supports: Independent coverage of Chrome 150 web-standards changes mentions text-fit scaling font-size to fill its box
sources.limitations: Brief summary; independent but secondary.

supports: Prior-art CSS-only fit-to-width technique using registered custom properties and container queries; evidence of workaround complexity and demand for a native property
sources.limitations: Author's technique writeup; approximate publication year.

supports: WPT results path for text-fit exists and was linked from the Intent to Ship
sources.limitations: Page is JavaScript-rendered and could not be fetched; per-test pass/fail and cross-engine results not captured.

supports: Tracking bug for the feature (referenced by ChromeStatus and Intent to Ship)
sources.limitations: Issue tracker is JS-rendered/auth-gated and could not be retrieved; status, open regressions, and test results not directly verified.

supports: Chrome 150 stable/Android/ChromeOS desktop updates rolling out in July 2026, confirming stable availability

sources.publisher: ChromeStatus / Chromium (CSS property popularity timeline, bucket 924)
supports: CSS property `text-fit` use counter (bucket 924; property-scope; exact-title match): first positive value on 2026-01-23 at 0.000003 (0.0003%) of measured page loads; peak 0.003303 (0.3303%) on 2026-06-03; 0.00058 (0.058%) at M150 stable (2026-06-30); 0.00091596 (0.0916%) at the latest point 2026-07-21; 180 timeline points; day30/day90/day180 launch checkpoints not yet available. Numerator = Chrome page loads on which the counter fired at least once; denominator = measured Chrome HTTP/HTTPS page loads across official channels and platforms.
sources.limitations: Property-scope counter that fires on any measured page load where the property is used or detected; not equivalent to unique developers, sites, users, successful task completion, satisfaction, production deployment, or end-user benefit. No WebDX feature/use-counter-enum mapping and no origin-trial counter exist for this feature; this property counter is the sole public telemetry and the closest available measurement, not exact feature adoption. Pre-stable positive values and the pre-stable peak (0.3303% on 2026-06-03) are unexplained (possible pre-stable-channel use, probing/detection, or low-volume noise). UMA reflects participating Chrome clients and the dominant milestone; enterprise and opt-out populations may be underrepresented. Cached timeline SHA-256 5400d1f3a6b12d21b1f900a8295f444139fb16a75cb452eff895709097331c42.

successes: Genuine, long-standing developer problem (responsive fit-to-width typography) with strong, traceable demand (long-running CSSWG issue, 110+ votes, influential proposer) and documented JavaScript-workaround pain [s1,s2,s10,s23].
Additive, compat-safe CSS property (no default behavior change) shipped enabled-by-default across desktop/Android/WebView in M150 with no reported breakage [s1,s16,s17].

Rigorous Intent-to-Ship review: an API owner explicitly challenged shipping with an open accessibility issue, and the team captured the ARIA/CSSWG context, perf tradeoff, and monitoring plan in ChromeStatus before the LGTMs [s1,s6].
Standards venue secured: CSSWG resolution to add the feature and place it in CSS Text Level 5, with spec text merged (PR #13616) [s3,s4,s10,s12].
Accurate, coordinated launch communication matching the actual rollout (New in Chrome 150 headline, release notes, web.dev summary, CodePen demo) [s16,s17,s18].
failures: Shipped Chromium-only with 'No signal' from both Gecko and WebKit and an unclosed TAG review; not Baseline, and the Chrome-only status was not flagged as a risk in launch materials [s1,s7,s8,s9,s18].

Shipped with an open, multi-WG accessibility issue (WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text) and no default safeguard; the accepted substitute is post-ship monitoring rather than a resolved mitigation [s3,s6,s11,s14].

Core design disagreements with the proposer/co-editor (grow-only vs grow+shrink; full font-size-relative scaling vs implementation-dependent; default 200% grow limit) were left unresolved, leaving the scaling model implementation-dependent and the most invested external expert publicly frustrated [s13,s14,s15].
No formal developer trial or Origin Trial, so no pre-ship task-completion, friction, framework-integration, or field accessibility evidence was collected [s1,s6].
MDN reference page and CanIUse entry absent at the stable launch of a headline CSS feature [s19,s20].
counterfactuals: A structured developer trial or Origin Trial before default-on would have produced field evidence on the one genuinely uncertain dimension (real-world zoom/accessibility behavior in viewport-dependent containers) and on the scaling-model divergence, instead of relying on post-ship monitoring [s6,s11].

Adopting kizu's default 200% grow limit, or prototyping the team's own heuristic before ship, would have closed the WCAG 1.4.4 concern proactively; the stated blocker (double-layout perf cost, uncertainty it 'works well') was a product judgment that accepted an open accessibility risk without first measuring the perf claim [s6,s11,s14].

Resolving or explicitly flagging the Gecko/WebKit 'No signal' state and the unclosed TAG review in launch materials would have made the Chrome-only/Baseline status transparent instead of implicit [s7,s8,s9].
Preparing MDN and CanIUse entries before stable would have given early adopters discoverable, accurate reference documentation at launch [s19,s20].
skillImprovements: Phase 04/06 rule: a 'No signal' from any major engine must be surfaced as an explicit interoperability/Baseline risk row (with date and last-touch) in the ship brief and launch materials; never omit it or treat silence as neutral.

Release-readiness gate for headline CSS features: require an MDN reference page and CanIUse entry to exist (or be tracked-to-close with a named owner) before stable; absence is a documented gap, not silent.

Prepare-to-ship 'accepted open issue' protocol: when shipping with a known unresolved cross-WG issue, the ChromeStatus entry must capture the accepted-risk rationale, monitoring plan, owner, and trigger conditions for a post-ship behavior change by default, not only when an API owner asks.

Experiment-phase decision prompt: for additive low-risk features, make the 'trial vs skip' decision explicit and record what field evidence is foregone and how it will be recovered post-launch (telemetry/accessibility monitoring).

Add a retrospective regression eval case for 'shipped Chromium-only with an open accessibility issue and unclosed TAG review' to verify reports do not infer success from shipment, usage, or press sentiment and do flag missing cross-engine and accessibility evidence.

Adoption-evidence rule: before a retrospective states a feature has 'no usage/adoption telemetry,' it must cache and cite the relevant ChromeStatus timeline(s) — CSS property popularity (matched by property/title token), WebDX/WebFeature popularity, legacy UseCounters, and origin-trial counters — and record the mapping status (exact / property-or-family / origin-trial / absent). The only honest negative claim is 'no mapped public counter found,' never 'no usage data exists.'

missingEvidence: Framework/library integration and production case studies: none found yet.
Support-issue taxonomy and regression/compatibility reports: too early to observe.
Other-engine (Gecko/WebKit) position or implementation: still 'No signal'.
Detailed WPT pass/fail and tracking-bug (417306102) contents: pages are JavaScript-rendered or auth-gated and could not be retrieved.
MDN reference page and CanIUse entry: confirmed absent at retrieval.
Unique-site, production-deployment, retention, and task-success breakdowns are not available; the only public telemetry is the property-scope `text-fit` CSS counter (bucket 924) [s28].
No predeclared adoption target in the explainer, intents, or launch materials, so the adoption outcome remains unscored despite available usage telemetry [s1,s2,s6].
day30/day90/day180 launch checkpoints for the property counter are not yet available (only ~3 weeks post-stable) [s28].
